subcategory: Perform perimeter network reconnaissance/scanning
description: Adversary uses commercial or free software to scan organizational perimeters to obtain a better understanding of the information technology infrastructure and improve the ability to launch successful attacks

subcategory: Perform network sniffing of exposed networks
description: Adversary with access to exposed wired or wireless data channels used to transmit information uses network sniffing to identify components resources and protections

subcategory: Gather information using open source discovery
description: Adversary mines publically accessible information to gather information about organizational information systems business processes users or personnel or external relationships

subcategory: Perform reconnaissance and surveillance of targeted organizations
description: Adversary uses various means over time to examine and assess organizations and ascertain points of vulnerability

subcategory: Perform malware-directed internal reconnaissance
description: Adversary uses malware installed inside the organizational perimeter to identify targets of opportunity

subcategory: Craft phishing attacks
description: Adversary counterfeits communications from a legitimate/trustworthy source to acquire sensitive information such as usernames passwords or SSNs

subcategory: Craft spear phishing attacks
description: Adversary employs phishing attacks targeted at high value targets

subcategory: Craft attacks specifically based on deployed IT environment
description: Adversary develops attacks that take advantage of adversary knowledge of the organizational information technology environment

subcategory: Create counterfeit/spoof website
description: Adversary creates duplicates of legitimate websites when users visit a counterfeit site the site can gather information or download malware

subcategory: Craft counterfeit certificates
description: Adversary counterfeits or compromises a certificate authority so that malware or connections will appear legitimate

subcategory: Create false front organizations to inject malicious components
description: Adversary creates false front organizations with the appearance of legitimate suppliers that inject corrupted/malicious information system components into the organizational supply chain

subcategory: Deliver known malware to internal systems
description: Adversary uses common delivery mechanisms to install/insert known malware into organizational information systems

subcategory: Deliver modified malware to internal systems
description: Adversary uses sophisticated delivery mechanisms to deliver malware and possibly modifications of known malware to gain access to internal organizational information systems

subcategory: Deliver targeted malware for control and exfiltration
description: Adversary installs malware specifically designed to take control of internal organizational information systems identify sensitive information exfiltrate the information and conceal these actions

subcategory: Deliver malware by providing removable media
description: Adversary places removable media containing malware in locations where employees are likely to find and use it on organizational information systems

subcategory: Insert untargeted malware into downloadable software
description: Adversary corrupts or inserts malware into common freeware shareware or commercial information technology products

subcategory: Insert targeted malware into organizational systems
description: Adversary inserts malware into organizational information systems and components specifically targeted to the hardware software and firmware used by organizations

subcategory: Insert specialized malware based on system configurations
description: Adversary inserts specialized non-detectable malware into organizational information systems based on system configurations specifically targeting critical information system components

subcategory: Insert counterfeit or tampered hardware into supply chain
description: Adversary intercepts hardware from legitimate suppliers and modifies the hardware or replaces it with faulty or otherwise modified hardware

subcategory: Insert tampered critical components into systems
description: Adversary replaces through supply chain subverted insider or combination thereof critical information system components with modified or corrupted components

subcategory: Install general-purpose sniffers on systems or networks
description: Adversary installs sniffing software onto internal organizational information systems or networks

subcategory: Install persistent and targeted sniffers
description: Adversary places within internal organizational information systems or networks software designed to collect network traffic over a continuous period of time

subcategory: Insert malicious scanning devices inside facilities
description: Adversary uses delivery services to deliver to organizational mailrooms a device that can scan wireless communications and wirelessly transmit information back to adversary

subcategory: Insert subverted individuals into organizations
description: Adversary places individuals within organizations who are willing and able to carry out actions to cause harm to organizational missions/business functions

subcategory: Insert subverted individuals into privileged positions
description: Adversary places individuals in privileged positions within organizations who are willing and able to carry out actions to cause harm to organizational missions/business functions

subcategory: Exploit physical access to gain access to facilities
description: Adversary follows authorized individuals into secure/controlled locations with the goal of gaining access to facilities circumventing physical security checks

subcategory: Exploit poorly configured or unauthorized systems exposed to Internet
description: Adversary gains access through the Internet to information systems that are not authorized for Internet connectivity or that do not meet organizational configuration requirements

subcategory: Exploit split tunneling
description: Adversary takes advantage of external organizational or personal information systems that are simultaneously connected securely to organizational information systems and to nonsecure remote connections

subcategory: Exploit multi-tenancy in cloud environment
description: Adversary with processes running in an organizationally-used cloud environment takes advantage of multi-tenancy to observe behavior acquire information or interfere with organizational processes

subcategory: Exploit known vulnerabilities in mobile systems
description: Adversary takes advantage of fact that transportable information systems are outside physical and logical protection and compromises the systems based on known vulnerabilities

subcategory: Exploit recently discovered vulnerabilities
description: Adversary exploits recently discovered vulnerabilities in organizational information systems in an attempt to compromise the systems before mitigation measures are available or in place

subcategory: Exploit vulnerabilities on internal systems
description: Adversary searches for known vulnerabilities in organizational internal information systems and exploits those vulnerabilities

subcategory: Exploit vulnerabilities using zero-day attacks
description: Adversary employs attacks that exploit as yet unpublicized vulnerabilities based on adversary insight into the information systems and applications used by organizations

subcategory: Exploit vulnerabilities timed with organizational operations tempo
description: Adversary launches attacks on organizations in a time and manner consistent with organizational needs to conduct mission/business operations

subcategory: Exploit insecure or incomplete data deletion in multi-tenant environment
description: Adversary obtains unauthorized information due to insecure or incomplete data deletion in a multi-tenant environment

subcategory: Violate isolation in multi-tenant environment
description: Adversary circumvents or defeats isolation mechanisms in a multi-tenant environment to observe corrupt or deny service to hosted services and information/data

subcategory: Compromise critical systems via physical access
description: Adversary obtains physical access to organizational information systems and makes modifications

subcategory: Compromise systems or devices used externally and reintroduced
description: Adversary installs malware on information systems or devices while external to organizations for purposes of subsequently infecting organizations when reconnected

subcategory: Compromise software of organizational critical systems
description: Adversary inserts malware or otherwise corrupts critical internal organizational information systems

subcategory: Compromise systems to facilitate exfiltration of data
description: Adversary implants malware into internal organizational information systems where the malware over time can identify and then exfiltrate valuable information

subcategory: Compromise mission-critical information
description: Adversary compromises the integrity of mission-critical information thus preventing or impeding ability of organizations to carry out operations

subcategory: Compromise design manufacture and/or distribution of system components
description: Adversary compromises the design manufacture and/or distribution of critical information system components at selected suppliers

subcategory: Conduct communications interception attacks
description: Adversary takes advantage of communications that are either unencrypted or use weak encryption targets those communications and gains access to transmitted information and channels

subcategory: Conduct wireless jamming attacks
description: Adversary takes measures to interfere with wireless communications so as to impede or prevent communications from reaching intended recipients

subcategory: Conduct attacks using unauthorized ports protocols and services
description: Adversary conducts attacks using ports protocols and services for ingress and egress that are not authorized for use by organizations

subcategory: Conduct attacks leveraging traffic/data movement allowed across perimeter
description: Adversary makes use of permitted information flows to compromise internal information systems which allows adversary to obtain and exfiltrate sensitive information through perimeters

subcategory: Conduct simple Denial of Service (DoS) attack
description: Adversary attempts to make an Internet-accessible resource unavailable to intended users or prevent the resource from functioning efficiently or at all

subcategory: Conduct Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks
description: Adversary uses multiple compromised information systems to attack a single target thereby causing denial of service for users of the targeted information systems

subcategory: Conduct targeted Denial of Service (DoS) attacks
description: Adversary targets DoS attacks to critical information systems components or supporting infrastructures based on adversary knowledge of dependencies

subcategory: Conduct physical attacks on organizational facilities
description: Adversary conducts a physical attack on organizational facilities

subcategory: Conduct physical attacks on infrastructures supporting facilities
description: Adversary conducts a physical attack on one or more infrastructures supporting organizational facilities

subcategory: Conduct cyber-physical attacks on organizational facilities
description: Adversary conducts a cyber-physical attack on organizational facilities

subcategory: Conduct data scavenging attacks in cloud environment
description: Adversary obtains data used and then deleted by organizational processes running in a cloud environment

subcategory: Conduct brute force login attempts/password guessing attacks
description: Adversary attempts to gain access to organizational information systems by random or systematic guessing of passwords possibly supported by password cracking utilities

subcategory: Conduct nontargeted zero-day attacks
description: Adversary employs attacks that exploit as yet unpublicized vulnerabilities not based on any adversary insights into specific vulnerabilities of organizations

subcategory: Conduct externally-based session hijacking
description: Adversary takes control of already established legitimate information system sessions between organizations and external entities

subcategory: Conduct internally-based session hijacking
description: Adversary places an entity within organizations to gain access for the express purpose of taking control of an already established legitimate session

subcategory: Conduct externally-based network traffic modification (man in the middle) attacks
description: Adversary operating outside organizational systems intercepts/eavesdrops on sessions between organizational and external systems and relays messages making them believe they are talking directly to each other

subcategory: Conduct internally-based network traffic modification (man in the middle) attacks
description: Adversary operating within the organizational infrastructure intercepts and corrupts data sessions

subcategory: Conduct outsider-based social engineering to obtain information
description: Externally placed adversary takes actions with the intent of persuading or otherwise tricking individuals within organizations into revealing critical/sensitive information

subcategory: Conduct insider-based social engineering to obtain information
description: Internally placed adversary takes actions so that individuals within organizations reveal critical/sensitive information

subcategory: Conduct attacks targeting and compromising personal devices of critical employees
description: Adversary targets key organizational employees by placing malware on their personally owned information systems and devices

subcategory: Conduct supply chain attacks targeting and exploiting critical hardware software or firmware
description: Adversary targets and compromises the operation of software firmware and hardware that performs critical functions for organizations

subcategory: Obtain sensitive information through network sniffing of external networks
description: Adversary with access to exposed wired or wireless data channels that organizations use to transmit information intercepts communications

subcategory: Obtain sensitive information via exfiltration
description: Adversary directs malware on organizational systems to locate and surreptitiously transmit sensitive information

subcategory: Cause degradation or denial of attacker-selected services or capabilities
description: Adversary directs malware on organizational systems to impair the correct and timely support of organizational mission/business functions

subcategory: Cause deterioration/destruction of critical system components and functions
description: Adversary destroys or causes deterioration of critical information system components to impede or eliminate organizational ability to carry out missions or business functions

subcategory: Cause integrity loss by creating deleting and/or modifying data on publicly accessible systems
description: Adversary vandalizes or otherwise makes unauthorized changes to organizational websites or data on websites

subcategory: Cause integrity loss by polluting or corrupting critical data
description: Adversary implants corrupted and incorrect data in critical data resulting in suboptimal actions or loss of confidence in organizational data/services

subcategory: Cause integrity loss by injecting false but believable data
description: Adversary injects false but believable data into organizational information systems resulting in suboptimal actions or loss of confidence in organizational data/services

subcategory: Cause disclosure of critical and/or sensitive information by authorized users
description: Adversary induces authorized users to inadvertently expose disclose or mishandle critical/sensitive information

subcategory: Cause unauthorized disclosure and/or unavailability by spilling sensitive information
description: Adversary contaminates organizational information systems by causing them to handle information of a classification/sensitivity for which they have not been authorized

subcategory: Obtain information by externally located interception of wireless network traffic
description: Adversary intercepts organizational communications over wireless networks

subcategory: Obtain unauthorized access
description: Adversary with authorized access to organizational information systems gains access to resources that exceeds authorization

subcategory: Obtain sensitive data/information from publicly accessible systems
description: Adversary scans or mines information on publically accessible servers and web pages of organizations with the intent of finding sensitive information

subcategory: Obtain information by opportunistically stealing or scavenging systems/components
description: Adversary steals information systems or components that are left unattended outside of the physical perimeters of organizations or scavenges discarded components

category: Maintain a presence or set of capabilities
subcategory: Obfuscate adversary actions
description: Adversary takes actions to inhibit the effectiveness of the intrusion detection systems or auditing capabilities within organizations

category: Maintain a presence or set of capabilities
subcategory: Adapt cyber attacks based on detailed surveillance
description: Adversary adapts behavior in response to surveillance and organizational security measures

subcategory: Coordinate a campaign of multi-staged attacks
description: Adversary moves the source of malicious commands or actions from one compromised information system to another making analysis difficult

subcategory: Coordinate a campaign that combines internal and external attacks
description: Adversary combines attacks that require both physical presence within organizational facilities and cyber methods to achieve success

subcategory: Coordinate campaigns across multiple organizations
description: Adversary does not limit planning to the targeting of one organization but observes multiple organizations to acquire necessary information on targets of interest

subcategory: Coordinate a campaign that spreads attacks across organizational systems
description: Adversary uses existing presence within organizational systems to extend the adversary's span of control to other organizational systems including organizational infrastructure

subcategory: Coordinate a campaign of continuous adaptive and changing cyber attacks
description: Adversary attacks continually change in response to surveillance and organizational security measures

subcategory: Coordinate cyber attacks using external internal and supply chain attack vectors
description: Adversary employs continuous coordinated attacks potentially using all three attack vectors for the purpose of impeding organizational operations

subcategory: Spill sensitive information
description: Authorized user erroneously contaminates a device information system or network by placing on it or sending to it information of a classification/sensitivity which it has not been authorized to handle

subcategory: Mishandling of critical and/or sensitive information by authorized users
description: Authorized privileged user inadvertently exposes critical/sensitive information

subcategory: Incorrect privilege settings
description: Authorized privileged user or administrator erroneously assigns a user exceptional privileges or sets privilege requirements on a resource too low

subcategory: Communications contention
description: Degraded communications performance due to contention

subcategory: Unreadable display
description: Display unreadable due to aging equipment

subcategory: Earthquake at primary facility
description: Earthquake at primary facility makes facility inoperable

subcategory: Fire at primary facility
description: Fire at primary facility makes facility inoperable

subcategory: Fire at backup facility
description: Fire at backup facility makes facility inoperable or destroys backups

subcategory: Flood at primary facility
description: Flood at primary facility makes facility inoperable

subcategory: Flood at backup facility
description: Flood at backup facility makes facility inoperable or destroys backups

subcategory: Hurricane at primary facility
description: Hurricane at primary facility makes facility inoperable

subcategory: Hurricane at backup facility
description: Hurricane at backup facility makes facility inoperable or destroys backups

subcategory: Resource depletion
description: Degraded processing performance due to resource depletion

subcategory: Introduction of vulnerabilities into software products
description: Due to inherent weaknesses in programming languages and software development environments errors and vulnerabilities are introduced into commonly used software products

subcategory: Disk error
description: Corrupted storage due to a disk error

subcategory: Pervasive disk error
description: Multiple disk errors due to aging of a set of devices all acquired at the same time from the same supplier

subcategory: Windstorm/tornado at primary facility
description: Windstorm/tornado at primary facility makes facility inoperable

subcategory: Windstorm/tornado at backup facility
description: Windstorm/tornado at backup facility makes facility inoperable or destroys backups
